content.claim: Cross-national quasi-experimental evidence from Hanushek & Woessmann and a 2021 meta-analysis of 75 countries and 21 assessment cycles shows that early tracking increases educational inequality with less clear effects on mean performance, though effect sizes and mechanisms remain contested across studies.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Studies use different data sources, domains, and inequality measures, making direct comparison difficult
Meta-analysis source sentence truncated in research notes, limiting full assessment of findings
Direction of effect (early tracking increases inequality) is consistent but magnitude varies substantially by study
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
